title: podostemaceae
intro: The Podostemaceae rank refers to a family of aquatic plants that are commonly known as the "riverweed" or "waterstems." This family is characterized by its unique ability to grow in fast-flowing water, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Members of the Podostemaceae family exhibit a range of adaptations that enable them to thrive in these environments, including modified roots and stems that can absorb oxygen and nutrients from the water. With over 100 species, the Podostemaceae family is an important component of freshwater ecosystems around the world.
